findingDetails.title: 019 packet docs diverge from shipped implementation; Files-to-Change table does not resolve to shipped files
findingDetails.claim: The spec Files-to-Change table and architecture prose describe an inline marker writer in memory-index.ts with a jobId field, but shipped code extracts it to lib/storage/maintenance-marker.ts (unlisted, labels field) and ships the .cjs under .opencode/bin/ not mcp_server/bin/; retry-manager.ts is an unlisted second call site.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Confirmed mcp_server/bin/ .cjs paths do not exist (ls ENOENT); located real .cjs at .opencode/bin/; confirmed maintenance-marker.ts exists and is imported by memory-index.ts:13 and retry-manager.ts:15; confirmed predicate ignores the renamed field so the rename is not a runtime defect.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: 019 docs written against an earlier inline design; marker later extracted into a shared reference-counted module and docs not re-synced. Shipped behavior is correct; only the descriptive map is stale.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: If the spec Files-to-Change table, architecture prose, and marker-shape description are updated to the shipped module/paths/fields, downgrade to resolved.

findingDetails.title: implementation-summary Known Limitation 4 is stale: embedding queue IS marker-protected per tick
Predicate unit-test fixture encodes obsolete jobId marker shape, not shipped labels

results.summary: REQ-001..004 all implemented and working in shipped code (marker writer + refresh + finally cleanup, fail-safe predicate, both guard sites, marker-dir parity). Files-to-Change table points to non-existent mcp_server/bin/ paths and omits the primary module maintenance-marker.ts and retry-manager.ts; marker field jobId->labels; TTL 60s->180s; impl-summary embedding-queue limitation stale.
Level 1 spec folder; no checklist.md required (exempt).
